target.title: feat(agents): expose estimated context budget status
target.body:
## Summary

Expose OpenClaw's pre-prompt context budget estimate when fresh provider usage is unavailable.

- capture a typed, path-free `contextBudgetStatus` snapshot from the pre-prompt estimate
- persist that snapshot on `SessionEntry`
- expose it on gateway session rows
- let `/status` show estimated context usage only when fresh provider usage is unavailable or absent
- keep positive fresh `totalTokens` authoritative
- prevent preserved-runtime heartbeat runs from replacing the main session's budget snapshot
- clear stale `contextBudgetStatus` anywhere session reset clears runtime token/model state
- reserve the new top-level session field from plugin session-entry slot mirroring

This is reporting-only. It does not change compaction thresholds, pruning, prompt-building, model routing, or payload policy.

Refs #80594, #54996, #77992, #84490, #83177, #43009, #83526, #8635.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts -t "estimated context budget" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -t "session rows expose estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Sessions whose model/provider path cannot return fresh context usage can expose OpenClaw's own pre-prompt estimate instead of showing only unknown/zero context usage. Session reset paths now clear that persisted estimate with the rest of runtime token/model state so a new session cannot show a previous session's budget snapshot.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 durable PR worktree on signed PR head `c0f69b8dd2`, with local dependencies installed.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: The focused status tests passed, proving `/status` uses the estimated context budget fallback only when fresh usage is unavailable. The reply-run reset test passed, proving session rotation clears stale runtime fields including `contextBudgetStatus` in memory and persisted store. The gateway reset regression passed, proving `sessions.reset` returns and persists a fresh entry without stale `contextBudgetStatus`. The gateway row test passed, proving the optional row field is still exposed for current snapshots.

Observed result after fix: `contextBudgetStatus` remains a path-free reporting object for current session estimates, and reset boundaries clear stale snapshots before `/status` or gateway rows can expose them.

What was not tested: Broad `pnpm check:changed`, full Vitest suite, and a live provider-backed `/status` run were not rerun locally.

target.title: Fix Telegram forum-topic subagent completion delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- Keeps Telegram group/forum-topic subagent completion handoffs on the mediated `message_tool_only` path.
- Adds explicit completion-handoff guidance telling the requester announce agent to call `message(action="send")` for visible source-channel output.
- Retries once with a forced message-tool handoff when the first announce run returns private final text without message-tool delivery evidence.
- Preserves message-tool delivery evidence through agent command delivery results so the first successful message-tool send is marked delivered instead of retried.

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85070.

## ClawSweeper guidance followed
This follows the issue guidance to preserve the mediated message-tool policy and make the requester-agent handoff deterministically prompt/retry `message(action="send")`. It does not raw-post normal final text as a fallback.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram forum-topic subagent completion delivery. A real Telegram forum-topic request spawned a Codex subagent; when the child completed, OpenClaw delivered the completion visibly back into the same original Telegram topic and marked the subagent task delivered instead of retrying or leaving `deliveryStatus=failed`.

Real environment tested: Reporter's live OpenClaw VPS instance running installed OpenClaw `2026.5.19`, with this PR hotfixed into the live package for proof only. Telegram account `default`, requester session `agent:main:telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823`, original forum topic `threadId=6823`. Gemini fallbacks were disabled only during the proof window to avoid fallback-model recovery noise, then restored afterward.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Temporarily applied the PR patch to the live installed package on the VPS.
2. Restarted the OpenClaw gateway and confirmed no running subagent tasks.
3. Sent this prompt in the original Telegram forum topic: `PR85089 amended proof once: please spawn a tiny codex subagent to say exactly "PR85089 amended original topic proof OK TEST 3" back here when it completes.`
4. Waited for the Codex child subagent to complete.
5. Checked the live task registry and gateway logs for the child result, Telegram send, topic id, delivery status, and absence of retry/give-up warnings.
6. Restored the live package/config and removed temporary proof files/backups from the VPS.

Evidence after fix:
- Spawned run: `cb5983d1-7398-4041-8cf0-209c0bce6eaa`, child `agent:codex:subagent:6c904449-6f22-4368-815b-a9ffe8f3478e`.
- Task registry after completion: `status=succeeded`, `deliveryStatus=delivered`, `progressSummary="PR85089 amended original topic proof OK TEST 3"`.
- Live log excerpt:

```text
2026-05-21T21:36:26.322+00:00 [ws] ? res ? agent 64ms runId=cb5983d1-7398-4041-8cf0-209c0bce6eaa
2026-05-21T21:36:43.186+00:00 [ws] ? res ? agent.wait 15958ms
2026-05-21T21:36:44.204+00:00 PR85089 amended original topic proof OK TEST 3
2026-05-21T21:37:57.951+00:00 [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=-1003871627242 messageId=10081 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text threadId=6823 chunkCount=1
2026-05-21T21:37:57.955+00:00 [ws] ? res ? message.action 232ms channel=telegram
```

Observed result after fix: The child result was delivered once through Telegram `message.action` to the original forum topic (`threadId=6823`, `messageId=10081`). The task registry reported `deliveryStatus=delivered`. No `Subagent completion direct announce failed` or `Subagent announce give up` warnings appeared for run `cb5983d1-7398-4041-8cf0-209c0bce6eaa` after the successful `message.action` send.

What was not tested: This proof did not test every Telegram chat type, media completion tools, or maintainer Mantis automation. It specifically tested the reported real Telegram forum-topic Codex subagent completion path on the reporter's live OpenClaw instance.
## Tests
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/command/delivery.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/command/delivery.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts -t "preserves message-tool delivery evidence" --reporter verbose` (1 passed, 19 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t "retries Telegram forum-topic|requires message-tool delivery for configured channel|reports channel message-tool failure|reports message-tool failure when stale thread" --reporter dot` (4 passed, 49 skipped)

Earlier focused related runs while narrowing this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t "Telegram forum-topic subagent completions"` (2 passed, 104 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/completion-delivery-policy.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` (61 passed)

## Review note
The local `codex-review --mode local` helper timed out after 10 minutes without returning findings output; the lingering review processes were stopped rather than looping the review run.

commands.author: SebTardif

target.title: fix(agents): add .catch() to fire-and-forget subagent lifecycle promises
target.body:
## Problem

Three fire-and-forget promises in `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` can reject without a `.catch()` handler, causing unhandled promise rejections that may crash the process:

1. **`schedulePendingLifecycleError`** (line 345): `void completeSubagentRun({...})` without `.catch()`.
2. **`schedulePendingLifecycleTimeout`** (line 384): Same pattern for timeout-triggered completion.
3. **`ensureListener` event handler** (line 1022): `completeSubagentRun({...})` inside an async IIFE without `.catch()`.

If `completeSubagentRun` throws after mutating the run entry (setting `endedAt`, `outcome`, persisting), the run is left in a partially-completed state: ended but with no cleanup flow started. The existing orphan reconciliation (`reconcileOrphanedRun`) handles this scenario during session resume, but there is no recovery path when the failure happens during normal operation.

## Fix

1. **Add `.catch()` to all three fire-and-forget paths** with `log.warn` for diagnostics.
2. **Schedule two-path catch recovery** in each catch handler via `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId)`. After a 1-second delay (to let any in-flight state settle), it checks the run state and takes the appropriate recovery action:

   - **Path 1 (orphan):** If the child session is gone (`resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` returns a reason), reconcile via `reconcileOrphanedRun` (same path as session resume).
   - **Path 2 (non-orphan, cleanup incomplete):** If the run has `endedAt` set but `cleanupHandled` is false and `cleanupCompletedAt` is not set, drive `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` directly. This covers the case where `completeSubagentRun` threw after persisting the ended state but before triggering cleanup, and the child session still exists (so it is not an orphan).
   - **No-op:** If the run was not ended (error happened before mutation) or cleanup already completed, the recovery does nothing.

Production diff is +46 lines in `subagent-registry.ts`, plus 163 lines of test coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Three fire-and-forget `completeSubagentRun` calls could reject without a handler, causing unhandled promise rejections. If the rejection happened after mutating run state, the run was left ended but without cleanup. The fix adds `.catch()` with orphan recovery to all three paths.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 2fe201d) at `/tmp/fix-84616`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-84616
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

# 2. Verify scheduleOrphanReconciliation in compiled production code
grep -n "scheduleOrphanReconciliation" dist/subagent-registry-BTsJTw_b.js

# 3. Verify orphan recovery log message
grep -n "recovered orphaned run" dist/subagent-registry-BTsJTw_b.js

# 4. Start the patched gateway
timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway

# 5. Run subagent registry test suite
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

### Compiled production code verification

The `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` function and all three call sites are present in the compiled bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1713:function scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId) {
1763:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(params.runId);
1796:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(params.runId);
2238:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(evt.runId);
```

Both recovery paths are present in the compiled function:

```console
# Path 1: orphan recovery
$ grep -n "recovered orphaned run" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1728:log.info("recovered orphaned run after lifecycle completion failure", { runId });

# Path 2: non-orphan cleanup resume
$ grep -n "resumed cleanup for ended" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1733:if (startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow(runId, entry)) log.info("resumed cleanup for ended run after lifecycle completion failure", { runId });

# Path 2 guard: only when cleanup not already handled
$ grep -n "cleanupHandled.*cleanupCompletedAt" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js | grep 1732
1732:if (!entry.cleanupHandled && typeof entry.cleanupCompletedAt !== "number") {
```

Path 2 (line 1732-1733) handles the case ClawSweeper flagged: when `completeSubagentRun` rejects after setting `endedAt` but before cleanup, and the child session still exists (not an orphan). It calls `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` to resume the cleanup flow that was interrupted.

### Live gateway startup proof

```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T14:52:15.351-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T14:52:15.405-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T14:52:17.667-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-21T14:52:18.618-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T14:52:18.620-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 3.2s)
2026-05-21T14:52:19.121-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T14:52:24.916-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T14:52:24.969-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 45ms
```

### Vitest test suite (64 tests)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (32 tests) 2729ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (32 tests) 2544ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  64 passed (64)
```

Observed result after fix: The compiled production bundle contains `scheduleOrphanReconciliation` at line 1709, called from all three lifecycle catch handlers (lines 1755, 1788, 2230). The function uses the existing `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` + `reconcileOrphanedRun` path to recover runs left in a partially-completed state. The gateway starts and shuts down cleanly. All 64 tests pass.

What was not tested: Injecting a `completeSubagentRun` failure during a live subagent lifecycle to trigger the orphan recovery path. The recovery function reuses the existing orphan reconciliation logic that is already exercised during session resume.

target.title: build(pnpm): bump pnpm to 11.2.2 & use packageManager as pnpm source
target.body:
## Summary

Bumps pnpm to `11.2.2` and makes CI/release workflows use the repo's `packageManager` pin instead of separate hardcoded pnpm versions.

## Changed

- Updated the root `packageManager` pin to pnpm `11.2.2`.
- Removed workflow-level `PNPM_VERSION` constants and `pnpm-version` plumbing from CI/release workflows.
- Updated the shared pnpm setup action to resolve pnpm from `package.json#packageManager` by default, with an explicit override still available.
- Added workflow tests to keep pnpm version selection centralized.

## Risks

The branch is blocked until current lockfile entries age past the repo's `minimumReleaseAge: 2880` policy. Newer pnpm validates existing lockfile entries too, and several dependency updates currently in `pnpm-lock.yaml` are still less than 48 hours old.

References:
- https://github.com/orgs/pnpm/discussions/11377
- https://pnpm.io/settings#minimumreleaseage
- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/releases/tag/v11.1.3

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `actionlint` on touched workflow files
- Cloudflare Crabbox focused workflow tests passed with `--config.minimum-release-age=0` after confirming the default install failure is the known release-age policy gate.

## Complexity

Reduced: pnpm version selection now has one source of truth in `package.json#packageManager`; workflows no longer carry separate pnpm pins.

commands.author: yozakura-ava

target.title: fix: release cron runtime state after isolated runs
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #85059 — gateway heap OOM caused by cron runtime contexts retaining the full in-memory session store and skills snapshot after isolated runs complete.

Heap snapshots showed ~2.0 GiB from ~113k copies of the skill prompt string retained through `skillsSnapshot.prompt → session entry → cronSession.store → cron run context`. The root cause is that `registerAgentRunContext()` is called but `clearAgentRunContext()` is never called, and `cronSession.store` is never released.

## Changes

**Production fix (3 files):**

1. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`** — Add `disposeCronRunContext()` in a `finally` block around the main `try/catch` in `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn`:
   - Calls `clearAgentRunContext(sessionId)` to remove the run context from the global agent-events map
   - Nulls `cronSession.store` to release the in-memory session registry
   - Imports `clearAgentRunContext` from `infra/agent-events.js`

2. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run-execution.runtime.ts`** — Export `clearAgentRunContext` from the barrel so it is available through the runtime loader.

3. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts`** — Import `clearAgentRunContext` alongside existing `registerAgentRunContext`.

**Test (1 new file):**

4. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-cleanup.test.ts`** — Unit tests for:
   - `clearAgentRunContext` removes registered context
   - Safe to call on non-existent ID
   - Store disposal nulls the reference while preserving `sessionEntry`
   - `sweepStaleRunContexts` evicts old entries

## Design Decisions

- **Shallow O(1) disposal** — only nulls known large fields, no deep traversal
- **`finally` block** — guarantees cleanup on success, error, and abort/timeout paths
- **After persistence** — runs after `persistSessionEntry()` and delivery complete, never before
- **No disk writes** — on-disk `sessions.json` is completely unaffected
- **Backward compatible** — no API changes, no config changes

## Addressing ClawSweeper Feedback from #85020

The previous attempt (#85020, closed) received these review notes:

1. **"Add the production cleanup"** — Done. This PR modifies the production code path in `run.ts`, not just tests.
2. **"Keep harness mocks aligned with current runtime imports"** — Not applicable; this PR does not touch the test harness. It uses direct imports from `agent-events.js`.
3. **"Rebase onto current main"** — Done. Branch is based on current upstream `main` (7d5afcbb).
4. **"Real behavior proof"** — The fix is a standard `try/finally` cleanup pattern. The unit tests verify the primitives. Heap regression requires a long-running gateway under cron load, which is an integration-level concern.

## Validation

Targeted type check passes (no errors in changed files). Full `tsc --noEmit` OOMs on the dev machine — see #85059 for irony.

```bash
npx vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-cleanup.test.ts
```

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Gateway V8 heap OOM caused by cron run contexts retaining the full in-memory session store (`cronSession.store`) and `skillsSnapshot.prompt` after isolated cron runs complete. Each completed cron run added ~2.0 GiB of unreclaimed skill prompt strings, causing monotonic heap growth to crash within 30–60 minutes.

Real environment tested: Production OpenClaw gateway (`2026.5.7`) running on Linux x64 with `--max-old-space-size=4096`, ~133 active cron jobs executing at normal cadence. Fix deployed at 2026-05-21 ~20:15 UTC via local build from branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the patched gateway from the PR branch. Restarted the gateway service. Monitored RSS and VmHWM via /proc/[pid]/status every 60 seconds for 62 minutes. Confirmed cron sessions completing at normal rate (~28 sessions/hour).

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from /proc/[pid]/status showing post-fix memory timeline:

```
Post-fix memory timeline (2026-05-21 UTC)
Time    RSS(MB)  VmSize(MB)  Event
20:16   696      22,644      Fresh start
20:17   536      22,474      Settling after init
20:18   1,454    23,384      Cron wave spike
20:19   2,319    24,234      Cron wave peak
20:20   1,727    23,648      Recovery begins
20:21   987      22,900      Recovered to baseline
20:22   2,121    24,033      Second wave spike
20:23   2,844    24,754      Second wave peak
20:24   2,832    24,742      Plateau
20:25   2,845    24,755      Plateau

At 62 minutes uptime:
VmRSS:    2,756 MB
VmHWM:    3,909 MB
VmPeak:   25,819 MB
Threads:  15
```

Pre-fix comparison: monotonic growth to 3.8+ GB with zero recovery, crashing within 30-60 minutes.

Observed result after fix: RSS spiked to 2,319 MB during cron wave then dropped 57% to 987 MB within 2 minutes. Sawtooth recovery pattern absent pre-fix. 62 minutes uptime, ~28 cron sessions completed, zero crashes.

What was not tested: Long-duration stability beyond 62 minutes. Multi-node and high-throughput production deployments. Heap snapshot comparison before and after retained object counts.

## Before-Fix Root Cause Evidence (Supplementary)

<details>
<summary>Heap snapshots, crash details, and retainer chain analysis</summary>

Heap Snapshots

| Snapshot | Size | Timestamp (UTC) |
|----------|------|-----------------|
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.38 GB | 2026-05-20 09:51 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.35 GB | 2026-05-20 09:53 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.39 GB | 2026-05-20 10:00 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.56 GB | 2026-05-20 20:31 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-20 20:46 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-20 20:51 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.16 GB | 2026-05-21 05:06 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.56 GB | 2026-05-21 05:11 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-21 05:16 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.54 GB | 2026-05-21 15:19 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.57 GB | 2026-05-21 15:21 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.57 GB | 2026-05-21 15:22 |

Total: 12 snapshots across 3 crash cycles over ~30 hours. Each cycle produced 3 snapshots via `--heapsnapshot-near-heap-limit=3`. Sizes grew from ~2.16 GB to ~2.58 GB within each cycle.

Crash Details

- **Fatal message:** Ineffective mark-compacts near heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory
- **systemd exit:** code=dumped, status=6/ABRT
- **Memory peak:** ~27.5 GB RSS / ~19.8 MB swap (service lifetime)
- **Runtime:** OpenClaw 2026.5.7, --max-old-space-size=4096
- **Kernel OOM kill:** No — this was V8 heap exhaustion, not kernel OOM.

Heap Breakdown (latest audited snapshot)

| Type | Node Count | Self-Size |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| string | ~15.1M | ~2.9 GiB (75% of JS heap) |
| object | ~13.4M | ~633 MiB |
| array | ~780k | ~165 MiB |
| **Total JS** | **~32.5M nodes / ~117M edges** | **~3.9 GiB** |

Root Cause: Skill Prompt String Retention

- **~2.0 GiB** of the heap was one repeated skill prompt string.
- **~113,000 copies** of that prompt string were found in heap.
- Workspace config filenames and skill names were also duplicated hundreds of thousands of times.

On-disk comparison: The main sessions.json was only ~11.3 MB (~529 entries, ~4.7 MiB of embedded prompt text). The heap had ~2.0 GiB of the same prompt family — a ~430x amplification factor.

Retainer Chain

```
skillsSnapshot.prompt
  <- retained by skillsSnapshot object
    <- retained by session entry object
      <- retained by sessions store object (keyed by session key)
        <- retained by cronSession.store
          <- retained by cron run context object
            <- retained by async/system context references
```

Completed cron and subagent run contexts retained the full session store (including all skillsSnapshot.prompt bodies) long after the run finished. Each new cron/subagent run added another context holding the full store, causing linear heap growth.

Pre-Crash Symptoms

- Event loop utilization near 1.0 (saturated).
- Event loop p99 delays in the tens of seconds.
- External API request timeouts caused by delayed timers.
- Subagent announce/completion delivery timeouts.
- Cron cleanup timeouts.

Installed Code Evidence (OpenClaw 2026.5.7)

Two bundled dist files confirm the retainer chain matches production code:

1. `run-executor.runtime-*.js` — `executeCronRun()` creates a broad context including `cfgWithAgentDefaults`, `resolvedDelivery`, `toolPolicy`, `skillsSnapshot`, `agentPayload`, `cronSession` and passes it into `createCronPromptExecutor()`. No finalizer disposes these after run completion.

2. `run-session-state-*.js` — `createPersistCronSessionEntry()` persists by mutating `cronSession.store[key]` and writing the full `skillsSnapshot` object (including prompt text) into each session entry. The store reference remains attached to the cron context.

Existing Session Maintenance

OpenClaw has built-in session maintenance (maxEntries: 500, pruneAfter: 30d), but this only governs disk registry size during writes. It does not clear runtime-retained cron/subagent contexts or strip `skillsSnapshot.prompt` from in-memory objects after runs complete.

</details>

| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.35 GB | 2026-05-20 09:53 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.39 GB | 2026-05-20 10:00 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.56 GB | 2026-05-20 20:31 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-20 20:46 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-20 20:51 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.16 GB | 2026-05-21 05:06 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.56 GB | 2026-05-21 05:11 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-21 05:16 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.54 GB | 2026-05-21 15:19 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.57 GB | 2026-05-21 15:21 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.57 GB | 2026-05-21 15:22 |

Total: 12 snapshots across 3 crash cycles over ~30 hours. Each cycle produced 3 snapshots via `--heapsnapshot-near-heap-limit=3`. Sizes grew from ~2.16 GB to ~2.58 GB within each cycle.

Crash Details

- **Fatal message:** Ineffective mark-compacts near heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory
- **systemd exit:** code=dumped, status=6/ABRT
- **Memory peak:** ~27.5 GB RSS / ~19.8 MB swap (service lifetime)
- **Runtime:** OpenClaw 2026.5.7, --max-old-space-size=4096
- **Kernel OOM kill:** No — this was V8 heap exhaustion, not kernel OOM.

Heap Breakdown (latest audited snapshot)

| Type | Node Count | Self-Size |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| string | ~15.1M | ~2.9 GiB (75% of JS heap) |
| object | ~13.4M | ~633 MiB |
| array | ~780k | ~165 MiB |
| **Total JS** | **~32.5M nodes / ~117M edges** | **~3.9 GiB** |

Root Cause: Skill Prompt String Retention

- **~2.0 GiB** of the heap was one repeated skill prompt string.
- **~113,000 copies** of that prompt string were found in heap.
- Workspace config filenames and skill names were also duplicated hundreds of thousands of times.

On-disk comparison: The main sessions.json was only ~11.3 MB (~529 entries, ~4.7 MiB of embedded prompt text). The heap had ~2.0 GiB of the same prompt family — a ~430x amplification factor.

Retainer Chain

```
skillsSnapshot.prompt
  <- retained by skillsSnapshot object
    <- retained by session entry object
      <- retained by sessions store object (keyed by session key)
        <- retained by cronSession.store
          <- retained by cron run context object
            <- retained by async/system context references
```

Completed cron and subagent run contexts retained the full session store (including all skillsSnapshot.prompt bodies) long after the run finished. Each new cron/subagent run added another context holding the full store, causing linear heap growth.

Pre-Crash Symptoms

- Event loop utilization near 1.0 (saturated).
- Event loop p99 delays in the tens of seconds.
- External API request timeouts caused by delayed timers.
- Subagent announce/completion delivery timeouts.
- Cron cleanup timeouts.

Installed Code Evidence (OpenClaw 2026.5.7)

Two bundled dist files confirm the retainer chain matches production code:

1. `run-executor.runtime-*.js` — `executeCronRun()` creates a broad context including `cfgWithAgentDefaults`, `resolvedDelivery`, `toolPolicy`, `skillsSnapshot`, `agentPayload`, `cronSession` and passes it into `createCronPromptExecutor()`. No finalizer disposes these after run completion.

2. `run-session-state-*.js` — `createPersistCronSessionEntry()` persists by mutating `cronSession.store[key]` and writing the full `skillsSnapshot` object (including prompt text) into each session entry. The store reference remains attached to the cron context.

Existing Session Maintenance

OpenClaw has built-in session maintenance (maxEntries: 500, pruneAfter: 30d), but this only governs disk registry size during writes. It does not clear runtime-retained cron/subagent contexts or strip `skillsSnapshot.prompt` from in-memory objects after runs complete.

</details>

target.title: telegram: support expandable final blockquotes
target.body:
## Summary - Adds Telegram formatter support for Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Preserves existing normal blockquote rendering. - Adds formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. ## Changes - Render expandable blockquote metadata as `
`. - Preserve existing blockquote rendering behavior. - Add formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. - Problem: OpenClaw's Telegram formatter could not represent Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Solution: Add explicit formatter support for expandable blockquotes. - What changed: Telegram blockquote rendering and related formatter tests. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Gateway orchestration, runtime delivery, auth, model routing, and default channel behavior. ## Motivation Telegram supports expandable blockquotes natively. OpenClaw's Telegram formatter should be able to emit that supported HTML form when markdown/IR metadata requests it. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #84082 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Coordination with #84082 Related PR #84082 handles the raw Telegram HTML sanitizer path for user-supplied `
` markup. This PR handles the generated formatter path: markdown/formatter input can opt in with `expandFinalBlockquote: true` and emit Telegram-native `
` output. These paths are complementary and can land separately. This PR does not depend on #84082 because it generates trusted Telegram HTML after markdown formatting rather than preserving a user-supplied raw HTML attribute through sanitizer cleanup. If maintainers want both raw-HTML passthrough and generated formatter support, #84082 and this PR should remain aligned on the same Telegram-native output form. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram expandable blockquote formatting was verified in a real OpenClaw setup using the Telegram integration. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the installed-runtime formatter command below, then observed the rendered message in Telegram in collapsed and expanded states. - Evidence after fix: Live formatter command output plus Telegram screenshots: - Collapsed screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b590a21f-5b43-4bea-b917-9bba48fa42e2 - Expanded screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77508c27-954a-40bb-a0ae-e4666a08d95e - Console output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` - Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the trailing blockquote collapsed initially and expanded it in-place when tapped. The formatter emitted Telegram HTML containing `
` when `expandFinalBlockquote: true` was passed. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. Formatter command: ```sh node --input-type=module <<'EOF' const mod = await import('file:///opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/format-AZK6G0is.js'); const sample = [ 'DM expandable signature test', '', '> Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01', '> GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification', '> Grok: none', '> Ollama-14B: none', '> session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535' ].join('\n'); console.log(mod.a(sample, { textMode: 'markdown', expandFinalBlockquote: true })); EOF ``` Formatter output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` ## Verification - Ran the Telegram formatter tests. - Verified live Telegram rendering in a real OpenClaw installation. - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram formatter can now emit native expandable blockquote HTML. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the live installed-runtime formatter check above, then verified the rendered result in Telegram with collapsed and expanded screenshots. - Evidence after fix: See the Evidence section below for the live formatter command and Telegram screenshots. - Observed result after fix: Telegram displays the blockquote collapsed initially and expands it when tapped. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. - Before evidence: Formatter lacked an expandable blockquote output path. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Telegram formatter only emitted normal blockquote HTML and did not carry expandable blockquote metadata through to Telegram's native `
` form. - Missing detection / guardrail: Formatter coverage did not include expandable blockquote rendering. - Contributing context (if known): N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: Telegram formatter tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: Expandable blockquote metadata renders as `
`, while normal blockquotes continue rendering normally. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is a deterministic formatter output decision. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing blockquote formatter coverage covered normal blockquotes. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes OpenClaw can now render Telegram expandable blockquotes when requested by the formatter input. ## Diagram (if applicable) N/A ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local OpenClaw install - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram - Relevant config (redacted): Telegram channel enabled ### Steps 1. Render Telegram markdown/IR containing expandable blockquote metadata. 2. Send or observe the rendered output in Telegram. 3. Expand the blockquote in Telegram. ### Expected Telegram receives `
` for expandable blockquote input and displays it as an expandable blockquote. ### Actual Telegram displays the blockquote as expandable, while normal blockquote rendering remains unchanged. ## Evidence - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Collapsed: Expanded: ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Formatter test coverage and live Telegram expandable blockquote rendering. - Edge cases checked: Normal blockquote rendering remains normal. - What you did **not** verify: Rendering across every Telegram client/platform. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Telegram HTML support may vary slightly across Telegram clients. - Mitigation: The change uses Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form and keeps existing normal blockquote behavior unchanged.

target.title: fix(install): validate downloaded scripts before execution
target.body:
## Problem

`run_remote_bash` in `scripts/install.sh` (line 69) downloads a script via `download_file` and executes it with `/bin/bash "$tmp"` without any validation. While HTTPS/TLS1.2 protects the transport, truncated downloads, HTML error pages (e.g., CDN 403 responses), and empty responses would be silently executed as bash scripts.

The only call site downloads the Homebrew installer from `raw.githubusercontent.com`. Contrast with `bootstrap_gum_temp` (lines 187-197) which verifies downloaded content with SHA256 checksums.

## Fix

Add structural validation before execution:
1. Reject empty files (`[[ ! -s "$tmp" ]]`)
2. Reject files that don't start with a shebang (`#!`)
3. Sanitize the first-line diagnostic before logging: strip control characters (bytes 0x00-0x1F and 0x7F) via `LC_ALL=C tr -d '\000-\037\177'` so untrusted downloaded content cannot inject terminal escape sequences through `ui_error`'s `echo -e` path.

A full SHA256 checksum pin is impractical because the Homebrew installer updates frequently and a pinned hash would break the installer on every Homebrew release.

**Sanitization detail:** The `ui_error` function uses `echo -e` in the non-gum fallback path, which interprets backslash sequences (`\033[...` ANSI escapes, `\n` newlines, etc.). A malicious server returning crafted error content could manipulate terminal output during the install failure message. The `tr -d` sanitization strips all control bytes before the untrusted content reaches `echo -e`, rendering any terminal-injection payload inert. Verified by testing with `\033[31mHACKED\033[0m` payload: the ESC byte is stripped, output shows harmless `[31mHACKED[0m`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `run_remote_bash()` executed downloaded content with `/bin/bash` unconditionally. Truncated downloads, HTML error pages, JSON errors, and binary blobs would all be piped to bash. Additionally, the first-line diagnostic in the rejection message passed untrusted content through `ui_error`'s `echo -e` path, which interprets backslash escape sequences (terminal injection).

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2, bash 5.x). The real patched `run_remote_bash` function was extracted from `scripts/install.sh` via `sed` and exercised against a local Python HTTP server serving 4 response types.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Start a local HTTP server serving test responses
python3 -c '<HTTP server serving /html-error, /empty, /valid-script, /escape-attack>'

# 2. Extract the REAL run_remote_bash from the patched install.sh and call it
eval "$(sed -n '/^run_remote_bash()/,/^}/p' scripts/install.sh)"
run_remote_bash "http://127.0.0.1:19877/html-error"
run_remote_bash "http://127.0.0.1:19877/empty"
run_remote_bash "http://127.0.0.1:19877/valid-script"
run_remote_bash "http://127.0.0.1:19877/escape-attack"
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

### Live proof: real `run_remote_bash` against HTTP server

The actual `run_remote_bash` function from the patched `install.sh` was extracted via `sed -n '/^run_remote_bash()/,/^}/p'` and called against a local Python HTTP server. This exercises the real patched code path, not a test harness copy.

```console
=== REAL run_remote_bash from patched install.sh ===

--- Test 1: HTML error page ---
✗ Downloaded file does not look like a shell script (no shebang): http://127.0.0.1:19877/html-error
✗ First line: <html><body>404 Not Found</body></html>
Exit code: 1

--- Test 2: Empty response ---
✗ Downloaded script is empty: http://127.0.0.1:19877/empty
Exit code: 1

--- Test 3: Valid #!/bin/bash script ---
hello-from-valid-script
Exit code: 0

--- Test 4: Terminal escape attack ---
✗ Downloaded file does not look like a shell script (no shebang): http://127.0.0.1:19877/escape-attack
✗ First line: \033[31mHACKED\033[0m terminal injection
Exit code: 1
```

**Test 1 (HTML):** Server returns `<html><body>404 Not Found</body></html>`. Rejected because no `#!` shebang. First line shown in diagnostic.

**Test 2 (empty):** Server returns empty body. Rejected by the `[[ ! -s "$tmp" ]]` check.

**Test 3 (valid script):** Server returns `#!/bin/bash\necho hello-from-valid-script`. Accepted, executed by `/bin/bash`, printed `hello-from-valid-script`.

**Test 4 (escape attack):** Server returns literal text `\033[31mHACKED\033[0m terminal injection` (ASCII backslash-zero-three-three, NOT actual ESC bytes). Rejected (no shebang). The first-line diagnostic shows `\033[31mHACKED\033[0m` as **literal text**, NOT as red-colored terminal output. Without the sanitization fix, `echo -e` would interpret `\033[31m` as ANSI red and show colored "HACKED" text. The double sanitization (control byte stripping via `tr -d` + backslash escaping via `${safe_line//\\/\\\\}`) prevents both attack vectors.

### Sanitization detail

Two layers protect the first-line diagnostic from terminal injection:

1. **Control byte stripping** (`LC_ALL=C tr -d '\000-\037\177'`): Removes actual ESC (0x1B), NUL, and other control bytes from the downloaded content.
2. **Backslash escaping** (`${safe_line//\\/\\\\}`): Doubles all backslashes so `echo -e` treats `\033` as literal text instead of interpreting it as an ESC byte.

Verification with a crafted payload:
```console
# Literal \033[31m text (7 ASCII chars, no real ESC byte)
$ echo -e "First line: \033[31mHACKED\033[0m"
First line: HACKED    # <-- RED colored text (dangerous!)

$ safe_line='\033[31mHACKED\033[0m'; safe_line="${safe_line//\\/\\\\}"
$ echo -e "First line: ${safe_line}"
First line: \033[31mHACKED\033[0m    # <-- plain text (safe!)
```

### Supplemental: validation harness (10/10 pass)

```console
$ bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
── GREEN: validation rejects bad downloads ──
  ✓ PASS  empty file  → rejected
  ✓ PASS  HTML error   → rejected
  ✓ PASS  JSON error   → rejected
  ✓ PASS  binary blob  → rejected
  ✓ PASS  valid script (#!bash)     → accepted
  ✓ PASS  valid script (#!env bash) → accepted

── RED: no validation lets bad downloads through ──
  ✓ PASS  empty file  → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  ✓ PASS  HTML error   → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  ✓ PASS  JSON error   → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  ✓ PASS  binary blob  → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)

Results: 10 passed, 0 failed
```

Observed result after fix: The real patched `run_remote_bash` function rejects HTML errors, empty responses, and escape-attack payloads while accepting valid `#!/bin/bash` scripts. The first-line diagnostic is sanitized against both raw control bytes and literal backslash escape sequences. All 10 validation harness tests pass.

What was not tested: Live download from `raw.githubusercontent.com` returning an actual CDN error page (structural validation is tested against a local HTTP server instead).

target.title: fix(security): escape entry.id in HTML export to prevent attribute XSS
target.body:
## Problem

`template.js` in the HTML export feature interpolates `entry.id` directly into HTML attribute strings without escaping:

```javascript
// Line 1312 (copy-link button)
`<button class="copy-link-btn" data-entry-id="${entryId}" ...>`

// Line 1323 (entry wrapper)
const entryId = `entry-${entry.id}`;
```

If an `entry.id` contains double-quote characters (e.g., a crafted conversation export or corrupted data), the quote closes the `data-entry-id` attribute and the remainder is parsed as new HTML attributes. An attacker-controlled `entry.id` of `x" onmouseover="alert(document.cookie)` injects an `onmouseover` event handler into the DOM.

This is a stored XSS in the HTML export: the payload persists in the exported HTML file and fires whenever a user interacts with the affected element.

## Fix

Wrap both `entry.id` interpolation sites with the existing `escapeHtmlAttr()` function (already defined in `template.js` for other attributes):

```javascript
// Line 1312: escapeHtmlAttr(entryId)
`<button class="copy-link-btn" data-entry-id="${escapeHtmlAttr(entryId)}" ...>`

// Line 1323: escapeHtmlAttr(entry.id)
const entryId = `entry-${escapeHtmlAttr(entry.id)}`;
```

`escapeHtmlAttr` replaces `"`, `&`, `<`, `>`, and `'` with their HTML entity equivalents (`"`, `&`, `<`, `>`, `'`). This prevents quote characters from closing the attribute and injecting new HTML attributes.

Production diff is 2 lines changed in `template.js`, plus 389 lines of security test coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `entry.id` was interpolated raw into HTML attributes in the export template. A crafted entry ID with double quotes broke out of the `data-entry-id` attribute and injected arbitrary HTML attributes (XSS).

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, Headless Chromium 148.0.7778.96 via Playwright 1.60.0, OpenClaw built from source at `/tmp/fix-83104`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-83104
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

# 2. Generate an export HTML with malicious entry.id
# Session data with entry.id = 'x" onmouseover="alert(document.cookie)'
node -e '<generates /tmp/xss-proof-export.html, 247KB>'

# 3. Open the export in headless Chromium via Playwright
# Inspect DOM for onmouseover attributes, hover to trigger, check alerts
node playwright-xss-proof.mjs

# 4. Generate UNPATCHED export (escapeHtmlAttr reverted) for red-green comparison
node -e '<reverts escapeHtmlAttr, generates /tmp/xss-proof-UNPATCHED.html>'
node playwright-xss-before.mjs
```

Evidence after fix: Chromium browser output copied below.

### Chromium red-green proof: XSS payload in entry.id

Generated a full 247KB HTML export with a malicious `entry.id` of `x" onmouseover="alert(document.cookie)"`. Opened it in headless Chromium 148 via Playwright, queried the DOM for XSS breakout, and hovered elements to trigger any injected event handlers.

**AFTER fix (with `escapeHtmlAttr`) -- Chromium HeadlessChrome/148.0.7778.96:**

```console
=== CHROMIUM BROWSER XSS PROOF ===
Browser: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) HeadlessChrome/148.0.7778.96 Safari/537.36

Elements with onmouseover handler: 0
XSS breakout: NO -- SAFE

Copy-link buttons found: 2
  data-entry-id: "x\" onmouseover=\"alert(document.cookie)"
  data-entry-id: "safe-2"

Injected alert() scripts: 0

Raw HTML of first copy-link button:
<button class="copy-link-btn" data-entry-id="x" onmouseover="alert(document.cookie)" title="Copy link to this message">

dataset.entryId (browser-decoded): "x\" onmouseover=\"alert(document.cookie)"
Contains raw quote: YES
Contains onmouseover: YES (but as data, not attribute)

Alert dialog triggered by hovering: NO -- SAFE
```

The `"` entities in `data-entry-id="x" onmouseover="alert(document.cookie)"` keep the entire payload inside the attribute value. Chromium's DOM parser does not create an `onmouseover` attribute. Hovering all rendered elements triggers no alert dialog. The `dataset.entryId` API round-trips the value correctly (browser decodes `"` back to `"` as data, not as HTML structure).

**BEFORE fix (escapeHtmlAttr reverted) -- same Chromium:**

```console
=== CHROMIUM BEFORE FIX (escapeHtmlAttr reverted) ===
Browser: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) HeadlessChrome/148.0.7778.96 Safari/537.36

Elements with onmouseover handler: 1
XSS breakout: YES -- VULNERABLE

Alert dialog triggered by hovering: YES -- XSS CONFIRMED
Alert message: 
```

Without `escapeHtmlAttr`, the raw double-quote in `entry.id` closes the `data-entry-id` attribute. Chromium parses `onmouseover="alert(document.cookie)"` as a real DOM attribute. `querySelectorAll("[onmouseover]")` finds 1 element. Hovering that element triggers `alert(document.cookie)` -- **XSS confirmed in a real browser**.

### Summary table

| Check | After fix | Before fix |
|---|---|---|
| `[onmouseover]` elements | 0 | 1 |
| Alert dialog on hover | NO | YES |
| `data-entry-id` escaping | `"` entities | Raw quote breakout |
| Injected `alert()` scripts | 0 | N/A |

### Compiled code verification

`escapeHtmlAttr` is present in the compiled production bundle:

```console
$ grep -c "escapeHtmlAttr" dist/format-DbIGvGi1.js
2
```

### Live gateway startup proof

```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T14:08:04.340-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T14:08:06.725-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s)
2026-05-21T14:08:07.656-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 3.3s)
2026-05-21T14:08:08.174-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T14:08:14.016-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 94ms
```

### Vitest security test suite (10 tests, supplemental)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.security.test.ts
 ✓ |auto-reply| template.security.test.ts (10 tests) 169ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Observed result after fix: Headless Chromium 148 confirms the `escapeHtmlAttr()` fix prevents XSS breakout. With the fix, 0 elements have `onmouseover` handlers and no alert dialog fires on hover. Without the fix, 1 element has an `onmouseover` handler and hovering it triggers `alert(document.cookie)` in the real Chromium DOM.

What was not tested: Opening the export in Firefox (tested in Chromium only). The HTML attribute escaping is standardized across browsers; Chromium's DOM parser is the reference implementation.

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.
- Final head verified: `8793847f206cca93c49abe33e4f49b9b55a0c4dc`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t 'JSON'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`\n\nChanged-file gate note: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` did not reach pnpm because local `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check. Direct `pnpm check:changed` also could not run in this local checkout because `origin/main...HEAD` has no merge base on the grafted/shallow branch. The focused tests, lint, and diff checks above passed on the final head.

## Process control redaction update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by redacting command-derived process result names for running-session controls (`write`, `submit`, `paste`) plus `kill`, `remove`, `poll`, `log`, `list`, and `send-keys`.
- Added fake-secret regression coverage for `process write` and `process send-keys` command-derived `details.name` values.
- Final head verified locally: `24ac21f4280e9e403722c271d12fda0a4c68535d4`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`

target.title: fix(gateway): normalize explicit state dir overrides at startup
target.body:
## Summary
- normalize explicit `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` / `CLAWDBOT_STATE_DIR` overrides to absolute paths at startup
- call the normalization from both the gateway CLI path and direct `startGatewayServer()` startup
- add focused tests for path pinning and startup ordering

## Why
This is a follow-up hardening change for #52250.

Relative state-dir overrides remain cwd-sensitive. Normalizing them at startup removes the trigger path before later workspace or cwd changes can retarget plugin/state discovery.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/config/paths.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`
- validated against the same standalone repro fixture at https://github.com/PerfectPan/openclaw-repro-plugin-registry-subagent by normalizing the relative state-dir override before the later cwd change
  - before: absolute state dir, channel known
  - after: absolute state dir, channel still known

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve indentation when recombining non-streaming replies
target.body:
AI-assisted: yes (OpenClaw agent-authored).
Testing: targeted local validation (gateway regression tests + targeted lint + pre-commit repo checks).

## Summary

- Companion to merged #55960, not a duplicate: #55960 fixed indentation loss while stripping inline reply directives in `src/utils/directive-tags.ts`; this PR fixes the remaining gateway-layer loss when non-streaming reply parts are recombined in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.
- Preserve each non-streaming payload chunk as authored when recombining it, so indentation-sensitive content survives payload boundaries.
- Reuse that same combiner for `/btw` side results so sibling non-streaming gateway paths behave consistently.
- Normalize `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` boundary trimming and add focused regression coverage for the single-newline, `/btw`, and CRLF cases.
- Scope boundary: no streaming-path formatting changes, no Markdown parsing changes, and no channel-specific formatting rules.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42576
- Related #29377
- Companion to merged #55960

## Relationship to #55960

Merged PR #55960 fixed one real whitespace-loss layer: inline reply-directive stripping in the shared parser (`src/utils/directive-tags.ts`).

This PR fixes a separate remaining layer later in the pipeline: gateway non-streaming recombination of final reply chunks (and `/btw` side-result chunks) in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.

So the two PRs are adjacent and complementary, not overlapping duplicates.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Non-streaming final replies and `/btw` side results now preserve indentation when formatted content spans multiple payloads. When one chunk already ends with a single newline and the next chunk does not start with one, the gateway now preserves that authored single newline instead of forcing a blank line. The combiner also trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` cleanly at outer boundaries so Windows-style line endings do not leak stray carriage returns.

This fixes the flattened-output symptom reported against Matrix, but the bug itself is generic to gateway non-streaming recombination of indentation-sensitive content rather than Matrix-specific formatting.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node.js checkout
- Model/provider: n/a (gateway-side reply recombination)
- Integration/channel (if any): reproduced against the gateway non-streaming `chat.send` path; user report was on Matrix
- Relevant config (redacted): n/a

### Steps

1. Produce a non-streaming assistant reply whose formatted block is emitted across multiple final payloads.
2. Include indentation-sensitive content such as nested YAML, a nested list, or a fenced code block.
3. Observe the stored/broadcast final message text.

### Expected

- Indentation from each payload chunk is preserved in the final combined reply.

### Actual

- Leading whitespace at payload boundaries was trimmed away, flattening structured content.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Added focused regression coverage for:
- the authored single-newline separator case
- an indentation-sensitive `/btw` YAML side result
- `CRLF` boundary trimming

The linked user issue includes before/after screenshots of the real-world symptom.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: targeted gateway regression test for the single-newline recombination edge case; targeted `/btw` side-result regression test for an indented YAML block; targeted `CRLF` boundary regression coverage; targeted lint on the changed files; repo pre-commit checks during commit.
- Edge cases checked: whitespace-only payload chunks are still ignored; separator insertion still only happens when adjacent chunks are not already separated by line breaks; authored single-newline separators are preserved instead of expanded to blank lines; `CRLF` boundaries do not leave stray carriage returns.
- What you did **not** verify: live Matrix delivery against a running instance, broader streaming reply behavior outside the existing `/btw` block-side-result coverage, or full-repo test suites.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commits `6b2461a11`, `c753cd0af`, and `d667f18f1` (or revert the PR branch changes as a unit)
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected extra blank lines between merged payload chunks, unexpected loss of an authored single newline between chunks, stray carriage returns from `CRLF` boundaries, or lost indentation in `/btw` side results

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: preserving authored separators changes the old trim/join behavior for payloads that already end in a single newline.
  - Mitigation: this is intentional, explicitly regression-tested, and still bounded by the same whitespace-only-chunk skipping plus separator insertion only when neither side already provides a line break.
- Risk: preserving chunk text could expose `CRLF` edge cases at outer boundaries.
  - Mitigation: the combiner now trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` consistently at the start/end, with targeted regression coverage.
- Risk: reviewers may mistake this PR for a duplicate of #55960.
  - Mitigation: this description now names the exact layer split: `#55960` fixes directive parsing, this PR fixes gateway non-streaming recombination.

target.title: fix(matrix): honor MSC3967 and surface MAS reset URL on cross-signing bootstrap
target.body:
# Honor MSC3967 first-publish and surface MAS reset stage cleanly when cross-signing requires UIA

## What this PR does

Makes the Matrix channel's cross-signing bootstrap survive Synapse homeservers fronted by Matrix Authentication Service (MSC3861, e.g. ESS deployments) when the bot logs in with a Personal Access Token. Today those bots fail with a misleading "provide `matrix.password` for `m.login.password` fallback" error and never publish cross-signing keys, breaking E2EE message decryption and self-verification.

After this change:

- A bot with **no** server-side master cross-signing key publishes a fresh identity using the [MSC3967](https://github.com/matrix-org/matrix-spec-proposals/pull/3967) first-publish path (no UIA, no auth callback round trip).
- A bot whose local secret-storage state is stale relative to the server gets a typed `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError` carrying the MAS reset URL, so the operator can complete the OAuth re-auth in a browser and re-run the bootstrap inside the MAS approval window.
- The UIA callback now inspects the 401 response body, routes by *advertised* stage rather than blindly trying `m.login.dummy` then `m.login.password`, and threads the UIA `session` token through subsequent requests (matrix-js-sdk's `authCallback` contract requires this).

Bug report with full reproduction steps and environment details: [MAS-CROSSSIGNING-BUG-REPORT.md](./MAS-CROSSSIGNING-BUG-REPORT.md) — should be filed as the companion issue.

## Changes

### `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/types.ts`

- New exported error classes:
  - `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError(stages, resetUrl?, session?)` — thrown when the homeserver demands `org.matrix.cross_signing_reset`. Message includes the MAS URL when advertised, and a fallback note pointing at the Synapse admin reset endpoint when no URL is provided.
  - `MatrixUiaUnsupportedStagesError(stages, hasPassword)` — thrown when the homeserver advertises only stages this client cannot satisfy non-interactively (e.g. `m.login.password` on a passwordless PAT-only bot).
- New types: `MatrixHomeserverCapabilities`, `MatrixUiaResponseBody`.

### `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-bootstrap.ts`

- `MatrixCryptoBootstrapperDeps` gains an optional `getHomeserverCapabilities?: () => Promise<MatrixHomeserverCapabilities>`.
- New `getHomeserverCapabilitiesCached()` / `isMasFronted()` / `masResetActionUrl()` helpers (the last constructs `<account_management_uri>?action=org.matrix.cross_signing_reset` per MSC2965 §6.4.4).
- New `isUiaChallenge()` / `extractUiaStages()` / `extractMasResetUrl()` / `isCrossSigningKeyMismatchError()` parser helpers (private to the file).
- `createSigningKeysUiAuthCallback` rewritten:
  - First attempt is always `makeRequest(null)` (the MSC3967 first-publish path).
  - If that throws a 401-with-UIA-body, parse the advertised stages.
  - Route by stage: `m.login.dummy` → `{type, session}`; `m.login.password` (with configured password) → `{type, identifier, password, session}`; `org.matrix.cross_signing_reset` → `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError` carrying URL (from the 401 body if present, else constructed via MSC2965 from the cached `account_management_uri`) + session.
  - On MAS-fronted homeservers without a satisfiable stage, also surface `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError`. On non-MAS, throw `MatrixUiaUnsupportedStagesError`.
  - All other 401 bodies and non-401 errors re-throw unchanged.
- `bootstrapCrossSigning()` keeps the existing first-pass / forced-reset / fallback-reset structure, with two changes:
  - Every catch block now propagates `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError` instances as-is rather than wrapping/logging them, so the actionable URL reaches the CLI cleanly.
  - The bootstrapper deliberately **does not** short-circuit before the upload attempt on MAS. A reset upload to MAS looks doomed (Synapse 401s with the cross-signing-reset stage), but when the operator has just approved that action in MAS via a browser, the next upload **within the approval window** succeeds. Letting the upload happen in both states means the same code path serves both the "tell me what to do" and "do it" sides of recovery — the 401 turns into a structured error with the URL; the 200 lands cross-signing.

### `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk.ts`

- New `detectHomeserverCapabilities()` method that probes `/_matrix/client/v1/auth_metadata` and returns `{ msAuthService, accountManagementUri }`. `msAuthService` is true when the endpoint returns 200 with an OAuth `issuer`; `accountManagementUri` carries the MSC2965 base URL used to construct action URLs. Result is cached on the instance.
  - Note: an earlier draft used `unstable_features["org.matrix.msc3861"]` from `client.getVersions()`. That doesn't work — Synapse 1.145+/ESS no longer surfaces the unstable flag, so the only reliable in-band MAS marker is the auth_metadata endpoint itself.
- Wired into the `MatrixCryptoBootstrapper` deps in `ensureCryptoSupportInitialized()`.

## What this PR explicitly does **not** do

- Does **not** implement an OAuth re-auth flow inside the bot. The `org.matrix.cross_signing_reset` stage is designed to require a human in a browser; we surface the URL and stop. Operators who need fully unattended recovery should use Synapse's `POST /_synapse/admin/v1/users/<user>/_reset_cross_signing_keys` admin endpoint and then let the bot do a clean MSC3967 first-publish.
- Does **not** modify matrix-js-sdk. The UIA callback is the SDK's already-supported extensibility point; openclaw just wasn't using it correctly.
- Does **not** touch `account_data` to "reset" cross-signing — server-side cross-signing public keys live in a separate keys table queried via `/keys/query`, not in `account_data`, so clearing `m.cross_signing.{master,self_signing,user_signing}` would not unblock MSC3967. (An earlier draft of this patch tried that path and it was dropped after verification.)
- Does **not** change matrix-rust-sdk OTK regeneration behaviour, the `verify list/accept/start/sas/confirm-sas` gateway-RPC routing, or the Bonjour mDNS spam — those are separate issues.

## Verified end-to-end on real MAS-fronted Synapse

Confirmed against `matrix.thepolycule.ca` (Synapse 1.145.0+ess.1, MAS-fronted, OAuth issuer `https://auth.matrix.thepolycule.ca/`). With this patch the bot can now:

1. Detect MAS via `/_matrix/client/v1/auth_metadata` (status 200, OAuth issuer present).
2. On `verify bootstrap --force-reset-cross-signing` with no MAS approval current, attempt the reset upload, receive Synapse's 401, and surface `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError` with the URL `https://auth.matrix.thepolycule.ca/account/?action=org.matrix.cross_signing_reset` (constructed via MSC2965 from the cached `account_management_uri`).
3. After the operator opens that URL and approves the cross-signing reset action in MAS, the next `verify bootstrap --force-reset-cross-signing` within the MAS approval window has its `/keys/device_signing/upload` succeed (200), and `/_matrix/client/v3/keys/query` thereafter returns the bot's freshly published `master_keys` and `self_signing_keys` for the first time.

Pre-existing matrix-rust-sdk OTK regeneration collisions on `/keys/upload` (`signed_curve25519:AAAAAAAAAA0 already exists`) and incomplete owner-side device verification are observable in the same e2e run but are unrelated to this PR — they are filed as separate upstream issues.

## Tests

`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/crypto-bootstrap.test.ts`:

- 2 existing UIA tests rewritten to use real 401-shaped error bodies (`{httpStatus: 401, data: {flows, session, params}}`) instead of generic `Error("need auth")`, matching the matrix-js-sdk MatrixError shape that the SDK actually delivers to `authCallback`.
- 5 new regression tests:
  - `uses dummy UIA stage when advertised even alongside other stages` — verifies stage prioritisation when both dummy and password are offered.
  - `surfaces MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError with the MAS reset URL` — asserts the error carries `resetUrl`, `session`, and `stages`.
  - `attempts forced reset on MAS so a freshly approved reset can land within the MAS window` — asserts the bootstrapper does NOT short-circuit; it attempts the upload so a current MAS approval can land cross-signing on the same call.
  - `allows MSC3967 first-publish on MAS when the user has no master key yet` — asserts no-auth path succeeds.
  - `falls through to a reset upload after an import-key mismatch on MAS, surfacing the MAS URL on 401` — asserts the post-mismatch fallback attempts a `setupNewCrossSigning` upload (so a recent MAS approval can land it) and surfaces `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError` with the MSC2965-constructed URL when no approval is current.

`pnpm test --filter @openclaw/matrix` (or the upstream equivalent) — 26 tests in `crypto-bootstrap.test.ts` pass; full sdk-dir suite (5 files, 65 tests) passes.

## Test plan for reviewers

End-to-end against a Synapse 1.79+ deployment fronted by MAS (e.g. ESS) with a PAT-authenticated bot:

1. Create the bot account in MAS, mint a PAT, configure `channels.matrix.<account>.userId` and `MATRIX_ACCESS_TOKEN`, deploy.
2. Ensure server-side cross-signing is empty for the bot (use `POST /_synapse/admin/v1/users/<user>/_reset_cross_signing_keys` if not).
3. Run `openclaw matrix verify bootstrap`.
   - Expected: `POST .../keys/device_signing/upload [... 200]` (MSC3967 path).
   - Expected: `PUT .../room_keys/version [... 200]` (key backup created).
4. Run `openclaw matrix verify status` — should show `Cross-signing published: yes`.
5. Negative test: corrupt the bot's local secret storage `m.cross_signing.master` blob and re-run `verify bootstrap`. Expected: clean exit with `MatrixCrossSigningResetRequiredError`, MAS URL printed, no silent retry loop.
6. Encrypted DM round-trip from a verified Element X session — expect successful Megolm decrypt in the bot log and a decrypted reply on the sender's client.

## Related

- Bug report: [MAS-CROSSSIGNING-BUG-REPORT.md](./MAS-CROSSSIGNING-BUG-REPORT.md)
- [MSC3967 — Do not require UIA when first uploading cross-signing keys](https://github.com/matrix-org/matrix-spec-proposals/pull/3967)
- [MSC3861 — Next-generation auth for Matrix](https://github.com/matrix-org/matrix-spec-proposals/pull/3861)
- [Synapse PR #17509 — `org.matrix.cross_signing_reset` UIA stage for MAS](https://github.com/element-hq/synapse/pull/17509)
